notes: Priestess finale cards. The booth fails in distinctive ways — silence where there should be sound, sound where there should be silence, the reel running empty, the visitor leaving mid-sentence. Each ending is a long shape the listener could write only because of who she had to be tonight.

finales.title: THE REEL RAN OUT
finales.flavor: The master reel has reached the leader-tape. The take counter clicks to a stop. The current visitor is mid-sentence. Elicia raises one finger — not a stop, just a wait. The visitor stops anyway. They say the rest off-mic, into the booth, into the air the booth has been holding for forty years. Elicia hears it. The tape will never have it. She nods, the way she always nods at the end of nights like this. Outside the booth window the cicadas are louder than they should be at this hour. The cassette gets a hand-written label that says SESSION ENDED IN ROOM ONLY. She files it in the third drawer down. The drawer has six others labeled the same way.

finales.title: THE LISTENER BREAKS
finales.flavor: Elicia is in the booth chair instead of the engineer's chair. She has been there for an hour. The mic is on. The reel is turning. She is not speaking. She has decided, for tonight, to let the room record her at last, and discovered she has nothing she's willing to put on tape — which is itself a thing she has just learned about herself. The lounge is empty. The kitchenette kettle has clicked off three times. The cathedral is on the other side of the wall, dark, doing whatever it does without her. When she finally stands up she leaves the tape running. The tape will run until it runs out. She does not stay to label it.

finales.title: THE SESSION ENDS EMPTY
finales.flavor: It is the end of the booked time. The lounge has no one in it. The booth has no one in it. The reel has nothing tonight worth keeping on it. Elicia spools it back. She thumbs the rewind switch and the tape goes the other direction, and her face does a thing she has not let it do in years. She labels the cassette BLANK · don't reuse and files it on the shelf with the other tapes labelled the same way. There are more than there used to be. She turns the booth lamp off. The cicadas keep doing what they do. The cicadas are not in this for her.

finales.title: THEY WALKED OUT MID-SENTENCE
finales.flavor: Three visitors left before they finished. Two of them were in the middle of the thing that mattered. One of them was almost at it. Elicia sits at the console with the headphones on. She listens to the silences they left behind — the pauses before they stood, the rustle of a coat being picked up off the lounge bench. The silences are not nothing. They are the wrong thing. She will keep the tapes. She will put them in the drawer marked UNFINISHED. The drawer is the largest drawer. Some nights she opens it just to remind herself the booth doesn't always work.

finales.title: THE CICADAS STOPPED
finales.flavor: The cicadas have stopped, all at once, the way they sometimes do when the riverbank gives. Elicia goes to the back door. The door opens onto the cathedral, and the cathedral is darker than it was when she came in tonight. Frasier is on the other side, holding the steamboat — finished now, smaller than she remembers, painted the red he meant it to be. They look at each other through the open doorway. Neither one speaks. The booth's red record-light is still on behind her. The cicadas do not start back up. The sound that's left in the air is the sound of the cathedral's pieces settling.

finales.title: THE SESSION IS OVER
finales.flavor: It is past the end of the booked time. The booth lamp shuts itself off on a timer Elicia set last summer because she knew nights like this happened. There are visitors still in the lounge who didn't get their turn. There are sentences on the reel that aren't yet sentences — half-thoughts, throat-clearings, the long quiets that should have become something. The night is finite. The booth was always finite. She files what's done. She locks the drawer. She writes a note on the index card by the front door that says SEE YOU FRIDAY in her own hand. Friday is in three days.

post_loss_behavior.note: Elicia's losses persist as residual Doubt carrying into the next run. What she didn't hear stays with her.
